title: Email Security
overview: Phishing remains the most common and effective method for compromising organizations and initiating data breaches. Despite its impact, email security is often overlooked. A strong email security program involves multiple layers of technical controls, user awareness, and reactive defenses.

study.overview: Phishing remains the most common and effective method for compromising organizations and initiating data breaches. Despite its impact, email security is often overlooked. A strong email security program involves multiple layers of technical controls, user awareness, and reactive defenses.
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
tips: Filters must be properly tuned—misconfiguration can lead to false positives or missed threats.
Example: A disgruntled employee attempts to email client data externally. DLP flags the message, blocks delivery, and alerts the security team.
This is one of the first layers of defense against phishing and malware campaigns.
Tools: Sophos Phish Threat, GoPhish, Trend Micro Phish Insight, PhishingBox
SHA256 is the preferred standard; MD5 and SHA1 are vulnerable to collisions.
